name: comm
description: Compare two sorted files line by line
readme:
Short:        Compare two sorted files line by line
Type:         util/cli
Architecture: m68k-amigaos >= 3.0
Uploader:     duncan (at) bowring (dot) us
Author:       University of California, Berkeley (ported by Duncan Bowring)
Version:      1.11

comm - Compare two sorted files line by line

Original author: University of California, Berkeley (Case Larsen)
Ported by Duncan Bowring using amiport (AI-assisted porting toolkit).
Project: https://github.com/bdgscotland/amiport

Cross-compiled with m68k-amigaos-gcc for AmigaOS 3.x (68000+).
Tested in vamos emulator and FS-UAE.

Usage:
  comm [-123f] file1 file2

  comm file1 file2       Show all three columns
  comm -12 file1 file2   Show only common lines (column 3)
  comm -23 file1 file2   Show lines only in file1
  comm -13 file1 file2   Show lines only in file2
  comm -f file1 file2    Case-insensitive comparison

Includes full source code (original POSIX and ported Amiga versions)
and PORT.md documenting every transformation applied.
porting_notes: Single-file EASY port (179 lines, pure C89). Standard Tier 1 transformations: pledge() stubbed as macro, exit(1) changed to exit(10) (RETURN_ERROR), LINE_MAX guarded with #ifndef fallback, __stack = 32768 cookie added, $VER string added. Review-amiga improvements: amiport_check_break() added to both comparison loops batched every 64 iterations (Ctrl-C handling); fclose(stdout) replaced with fflush(stdout) to avoid closing shell handle; line buffers made static to reduce stack pressure. Perf-optimizer: printf replaced with fputs (avoids format dispatch overhead on 7MHz 68000); strcoll replaced with strcmp (libnix has no real locale support). Memory audit CLEAN -- no dynamic allocation, no leaks, all exit paths safe. 30/30 FS-UAE tests pass including flag combinations, error paths, edge cases (empty files, single-line files, no-common-lines), long lines, stress test with 200-line files, and real-world use cases.
known_limitations: strcoll() collation depends on AmigaOS locale; for non-ASCII filenames results may differ from POSIX sort order. -f flag uses tolower() which only handles ASCII case folding (no locale-aware case comparison on AmigaOS 3.x).
